. This chapter describes the beginning of Daniel's last vision, which continues to the end of the book. The time before everything is accomplished will be long; and much of it has not yet been fulfilled. Christ appeared to Daniel in a glorious form, and we should be moved to think highly and honorably of him. Let us admire his condescension for us and our salvation. There remained no strength in Daniel. The greatest and best of men cannot bear the full revelations of God's glory; for no man can see it and live; but glorified saints see Christ as he is, and can bear the sight. However dreadful Christ may appear to those with convictions of sin, there is enough in his word to quiet their spirits.

Whenever we enter into fellowship with God, we should have a due sense of the infinite distance between us and the holy God. How can we, who are dust and ashes, speak to the Lord of glory? Nothing is more likely or more effective to revive the drooping spirits of the saints than to be assured of God's love for them. From the very first day we begin to look toward God in a way of duty, he is ready to meet us in the way of mercy. God is this ready to hear prayer. When the angel had told the prophet of the things to come, he was to return and oppose the decrees of the Persian kings against the Jews. The angels are employed as God's ministering servants. Though much was done against the Jews by the kings of Persia, God permitting it, much more mischief would have been done if God had not prevented it. He will now more fully show what were God’s purposes, of which the prophecies form an outline; and we are concerned to study what is written in these Scriptures of truth, for they belong to our everlasting peace. While Satan and his angels, and evil counselors, excite princes to mischief against the church, we may rejoice that Christ our Prince, and all his mighty angels, act against our enemies; but we ought not to expect many to favor us in this evil world. Yet the whole counsel of God shall be established; and let each one pray, Lord Jesus, be our righteousness now, and you will be our everlasting confidence, through life, in death, at the day of judgment, and for evermore.
